zh.lifeSummary: 當Georg Wilhelm Kell於1681年3月16日出生在德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 維德雷斯多福時，他的父親Hans Peter Kalhl31歲，母親Anna Maria Fackert31歲。他於1698年與Amelia Christina Fritz在海因里希_海普洛文次維德雷斯多福結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1744年6月30日在美國紐約州武爾斯特武爾斯特享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Georg Wilhelm Kell于1681年3月16日出生在德国海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 維德雷斯多福时，他的父亲Hans Peter Kalhl31岁，母亲Anna Maria Fackert31岁。他于1698年与Amelia Christina Fritz在海因里希_海普洛文次維德雷斯多福结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1744年6月30日在美国纽约州武爾斯特武爾斯特享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: Kell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from Middle High German and Middle Low German kelle ‘trowel’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker or user of such tools.
German: habitational name from any of the places, especially in Rhineland, named Kell, from Middle Low German kel (a field name denoting swampy land), or from kelle ‘steep path, ravine’ (see Kelle ).

English (northern): from the Old Norse personal name Kel, Kæl, shortened from Áskell (from Ásketill), Ulkel (from Ulfkil), or similar, rather than from the single-element name Ketill (see Kettle ) or its descendants, the shortening of which in Scandinavia occurs later.

variants.name: Kellman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Ari, Chaya, Meyer, Yaakov.
Americanized form of German Kellmann: elaborated variant of Kell 2, with the addition of -mann ‘man’. Compare Kelman .

variants.name: Kells
meanings: Scottish and Irish: from a personal name based on Old Norse ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’, with post-medieval excrescent English -s. In Scotland, it may also be of topographic origin, from a place called as ‘the cauldron’.
English: variant of Kell , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Kelle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Klaus.
German: topographic name from the dialect word Kelle ‘steep path, ravine’.

variants.name: Kellenberger
meanings: German: habitational name for someone from a place called Kellenberg in Rhineland (see   Kell   2) or Lower Saxony.

variants.name: Kellen
meanings: German: habitational name from a place so named in Rhineland (see   Kell   2).

nameMeanings.name: Georg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Wilhelm
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from wil ‘will’ + helm ‘helmet’; an ancient royal name. See also William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Georg Wilhelm Kell was born on 16 March 1681, in Rengsdorf, Neuwied, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, his father, Hans Peter Kalhl, was 31 and his mother, Anna Maria Fackert, was 31. He married Amelia Christina Fritz in 1698, in Rengsdorf, Neuwied, Rhineland, Prussia. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 30 June 1744, in Ulster, Ulster, New York, United States, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Georg Wilhelm Kell est né le 16 mars 1681, à Rengsdorf, Landkreis Neuwied, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne, son père, Hans Peter Kalhl, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Anna Maria Fackert, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Amelia Christina Fritz en 1698, à Rengsdorf, Neuwied, Rhénanie, Prusse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 30 juin 1744, à Ulster, Ulster, New York, États-Unis, à l’âge de 63 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Georg Wilhelm Kellが1681年3月16日にドイツルヘインランド-プファルズランドクレイス ネウウィエドレングスドルフで生まれた時，父Hans Peter Kalhlは31歳，母Anna Maria Fackertは31歳であった。1698年にプレウssンルヘインプロヴィンズネウウィエドレングスドルフでAmelia Christina Fritzと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1744年6月30日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州ウルスターウルスターで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Georg Wilhelm Kell(이)가 1681년 3월 16일 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 랜드크레이스 니우위드 렌그즈도르프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hans Peter Kalhl(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Maria Fackert(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1698년 프레우ss 리인프로빈츠 니우위드 렌그즈도르프에서 Amelia Christina Fritz(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1744년 6월 30일 미국 뉴욕 주 울스터 울스터에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Amelia Christina Fritz в 1698 г. в местности ренгсдорф, неувиед, раинпровинз, Пруссия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери.
